text.title: ES Snapshots Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding ES snapshot policy + storage.

rule.statement: Snapshots MUST be treated as a performance cache only. The system MUST reconstruct correct aggregate state from offset 0 without any snapshot present. Tests MUST cover the "snapshot deleted" path.
rule.rationale: Snapshot rot is silent; treating snapshots as truth means a bug in `apply` produces wrong state forever.

rule.statement: Snapshots MUST be taken every N events (recommended N ∈ [50, 100]). Store `(stream_id, snapshot_version, serialized_state)`. Loader fetches latest snapshot, then replays only events with version > snapshot_version.
rule.rationale: N too small thrashes storage; N too large erases the latency win. The 50–100 band is the empirical sweet spot.

rule.statement: When event-schema version bumps (a field rename, removal, or semantic change), ALL snapshots for affected aggregate types MUST be invalidated before the new code deploys. An invalidation script MUST exist and be runnable in CI.
rule.rationale: Stale snapshots restore old state shape; new `apply` handlers expect new shape; mismatch causes runtime errors or silent data loss.

rule.statement: Snapshot deserialization errors (corruption, version mismatch) MUST NOT crash the load path. The loader MUST fall back to replay-from-offset-0 and log the snapshot failure for repair.
rule.rationale: Production cannot afford a snapshot rot to take down aggregate loading; log-replay is the always-available fall-back.

rule.statement: Snapshot rows MUST carry the event-schema version they were built against. Loader MUST compare schema version to current; on mismatch, treat the snapshot as missing.
rule.rationale: Without version awareness, the loader cannot distinguish a fresh snapshot from a stale one across schema migrations.
